pains.summary: Redwood was operating blind with basic last-touch attribution and couldn't make informed decisions about pipeline
pains.quote: a year and a half ago i could go to vista and we're also we're split owned by warburg as well so i have the luxury of two boards uh to talk to uh a year and a half ago telling them that we're operating off of uh basic last touch um was okay because we said hey there's all this opportunity and we were mostly inbound before it is not flying anymore and we're heavy inbound as well and now we have kind of some channel you know we're we really are a little bit blind and making some of the decisions that we uh that we need to
pains.speaker: Bryan Urioste

pains.summary: Data complexity at Redwood made implementation a grind compared to other customers
pains.quote: I think one difference between some of our other customers and our engagement here, um is that uh i think data itself is a little complicated um uh when it comes to like the number of emails that redwood sends the number of uh how content is viewed you know like going through these use cases that we have gone through in this last week or so um has opened our eyes on like how data is being ingested and you know what complexities we need to take into consideration so these are this is the kind of involvement you know it was needed for us to like get to a place where we are able to make sense of you know what we're seeing in the platform it was a grind for a week
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Email marketing getting 50%+ attribution credit seems surprisingly high and may indicate loose definition of engagement
pains.quote: I mean am I the only one that surprised that 50% of our attribution is coming from
pains.speaker: Bryan Urioste

pains.summary: Historical data issues from acquisitions and multiple Salesforce systems creating dirty data problems
pains.quote: i think a lot of the root cause of some of the problems that we're having is because of dirty data smashed together salesforce systems acquisitions that kind of thing
pains.speaker: Frank

pains.summary: HubSpot page visits not fully captured, creating gaps in first-touch attribution
pains.quote: some cases we are seeing like um submitting the form as like the first touch this may be because we are not getting all of the HubSpot page visits uh historically
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Lead statuses not properly stamped historically, breaking funnel tracking
pains.quote: We did not have lead statuses before. So first touch and statuses were not a thing. So statuses were not getting stamped at the first touch level.
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

pains.summary: Form submit data did not migrate during initial implementation, causing incorrect journey attribution
pains.quote: ta  just  did  not  make  it  over  during  the  initial  implementation  um  so  that's  why  form  submit  is  showing  up  for  so  many  versus  what  potentially  happened  right  before  it
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

pains.summary: Salesforce hiccups prevented leads from being moved back to 'new' status, causing data integrity issues
pains.quote: moved  to  the  new  statuses  people  didn't  necessarily  start  at  new  they  could  have  been  attempting  contact  they  could  have  been  at  sql  there's  like  other  values  that  are  further  down  the  funnel  that  we  didn't  necessarily  make  them  go  back  to  new  just  because  they're  a  bunch  of  hiccups  on  the  salesforce  side  to  do  that  but  um  we  might  lose  out  on  people  that  actually  made  it  into  those  opportunities  this  is  just  one  of  the  pains
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

pains.summary: Campaigns on new business side not always including Mops team, causing operational gaps
pains.quote: there's  probably  also  opportunity  there  because  I  know  there's  been  a  lot  of  campaigns  that  haven't  always  included  the  Mops  team  on  the  new  business  side
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

pains.summary: Zoom meetings being incorrectly counted as page visits in attribution data
pains.quote: page  visits  because  they're  attending  meetings
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

pains.summary: Salesforce campaigns not properly built out, requiring ad hoc programs to track lead sources
pains.quote: a  lot  of  things  that  they're  doing  they're  not  necessarily  building  out  with  us  um  so  i've  been  building  out  ad  hoc  programs  in  order  to  determine  where  they're  coming  from
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

pains.summary: Previous year was messy for data/operations
pains.quote: last  year  was  kind  of  a  mess  anyway
pains.speaker: Mani Sandhu

tools_switched_from.reason: Moving to Marketo for better campaign tracking and data cleanliness
tools_switched_from.quote: we're moving over to Marketo, so we've already have limited access into the HubSpot instances
tools_switched_from.speaker: Andres Vega

tools_switched_from.reason: Not sufficient for current outbound + channel mix, leaving leadership 'blind'
tools_switched_from.quote: a year and a half ago telling them that we're operating off of uh basic last touch um was okay because we said hey there's all this opportunity and we were mostly inbound before it is not flying anymore
tools_switched_from.speaker: Bryan Urioste

political_tussles.tension_label: Attribution credit disputes and campaign classification
political_tussles.summary: Debate over whether form submissions should get attribution credit vs. the channel that drove to the form
political_tussles.quote: if something drove to the demo that gets credit, not the demo. Is that would that just show up in the journey?
political_tussles.speaker: Mani Sandhu

political_tussles.tension_label: BDR/SDR sequence attribution vs. marketing touchpoints
political_tussles.summary: Discussion about whether BDR sequences are getting too much credit because they're the last touch before discovery
political_tussles.quote: because they go into outreach sequences almost immediately within like 10 minutes of entering our system. So I would expect there to be more than just two.
political_tussles.speaker: Mani Sandhu

political_tussles.tension_label: Data cleanup prioritization and system fixes
political_tussles.summary: RevOps hasn't fixed lead status transitions that break funnel tracking, and campaign type additions are at bottom of their list
political_tussles.quote: the team hasn't really been giving us period costs aside from events but even then we don't always get a number
political_tussles.speaker: Mani Sandhu

political_tussles.summary: New business campaigns not always including Mops team, creating operational friction
political_tussles.quote: there's  probably  also  opportunity  there  because  I  know  there's  been  a  lot  of  campaigns  that  haven't  always  included  the  Mops  team  on  the  new  business  side
political_tussles.speaker: Mani Sandhu

contrarians.claim: Being comfortable with 'a grind' and MVP-first agile approach to attribution implementation
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors pitch quick time-to-value; Redwood explicitly accepts a slower, iterative path
contrarians.quote: We're very comfortable operating in, you know, kind of an agile mindset. Let's get to MVP first, knowing that there's much bigger gains to be had later, that's fine. And I know we've kind of cut the use case list down significantly, and then significantly again, and again, but just wanted to get your take on that.
contrarians.speaker: Bryan Urioste

contrarians.claim: Email marketing getting 50%+ attribution credit might actually be correct due to late Marketo launch
contrarians.why_unusual: Typically high email attribution would trigger alarm bells, but context makes it plausible
contrarians.quote: hope i'm surprised still that it's 50 since we only launched marchetto in november um i'm
contrarians.speaker: Mani Sandhu

contrarians.claim: Form submissions should NOT get attribution credit - only what drove to the form
contrarians.why_unusual: Many platforms credit the conversion point; Redwood wants to strip form-submit credit entirely
contrarians.quote: they should not be a campaign touch if that's going to get all of the credit.
contrarians.speaker: Mani Sandhu

contrarians.claim: Direct traffic as first touch is acceptable noise that can't be fully solved
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors promise to solve direct traffic attribution; Redwood team accepts it as unsolvable nuance
contrarians.quote: you know, there's going to be nuances that we can't solve for. Like in this case, technically it should be Google just because we know that's how they found us. Chances are they're never directly coming to us.
contrarians.speaker: Mani Sandhu

revsure_problems.problem: Mutiny experience showing as first touch when it should require prior website visit
revsure_problems.quote: How can a first touch point be a mutiny experience when to get the mutiny experience, you had to have visited the website first?
revsure_problems.speaker: Frank

revsure_problems.problem: Confusion about what 'progressed to new' means vs. lead creation - whether it's becoming known or behavioral score change
revsure_problems.quote: to new, is that just because that's our current progress stage? So in this case, if it's progressing to new, does that just mean people, the journeys that were taken in order to become known in our system?
revsure_problems.speaker: Mani Sandhu

revsure_problems.problem: GA client ID and UTM parameters not properly stitching to show pre-form submission journey
revsure_problems.quote: those UTM parameters are not, this is from September, those would have been programmatically put into Salesforce. That wouldn't have been put in manually by an SDR.
revsure_problems.speaker: Frank

revsure_problems.problem: Form submit data did not migrate during initial implementation
revsure_problems.quote: ta  just  did  not  make  it  over  during  the  initial  implementation  um  so  that's  why  form  submit  is  showing  up  for  so  many  versus  what  potentially  happened  right  before  it
revsure_problems.speaker: Mani Sandhu

revsure_problems.problem: Zoom meetings incorrectly appearing as page visits in attribution
revsure_problems.quote: page  visits  because  they're  attending  meetings
revsure_problems.speaker: Mani Sandhu

revsure_problems.problem: Organic search traffic being bucketed as synthetic campaign without UTM clarity
revsure_problems.quote: is  it  that  it  was  organic  search  and  then  it  came  through  google  And  then  that's  the  article.  Is  that  a  Revsure  thing  that  just  knows  that  it  came  through  Google  but  didn't  have  a  UTM  and  therefore  it  should  bucket  it  as  a  synthetic
revsure_problems.speaker: Mani Sandhu

best_about_revsure.summary: Platform's strength as both data science tool and reporting platform for proving/disproving hypotheses
best_about_revsure.quote: it is both a data science um you know tool measurement platform and also like a reporting platform where you know it serves the purpose of here are the numbers um of uh you know opportunities that are
best_about_revsure.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

best_about_revsure.summary: Commitment and responsiveness of RevSure team, including recent staffing adjustment
best_about_revsure.quote: we're committed to get you to that point uh brian and team and that's why ram recently joined us and that's why he's And I've been watching the Slack channel very closely, so forth and so on. So that's kind of why we made that even adjustment even a month ago leading into this to kind of help us, you know, moving forward here.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Alex Cox

best_about_revsure.summary: Quick turnaround on new views based on other Vista portfolio company requirements
best_about_revsure.quote: we just rolled out like a set of dashboards for example from one vista account that answers a lot of other questions black and white right that are even new things that came in the past like month or so right so it's an evolving thing
best_about_revsure.speaker: Alex Cox

immediate.win: Top 5 pipeline-producing campaigns view now functional for RMJ new logo
immediate.quote: what we're looking at on the top right here is we're looking at the top five pipeline producing campaigns. So highest five. campaign names based on generated pipeline volume. So this widget automatically sorts it for you.
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Channel classification improvements rolling out weekly with Andres/Mani
immediate.quote: i meet with andre simani like every monday right and we just have continued working continue through the channel classification just making uh improvements every week
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Mass filter application feature rolled out to simplify dashboard management
immediate.quote: one thing we rolled out, Frank, is now you can mass apply filters. like the top level filters you can mass apply.
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Direct channel and chat campaign touch creation implemented
immediate.quote: we've created the direct channel so when we don't see touch points we have the direct channel and then we've also done um chat right so chat has been created from like the logic that you gave me
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Working to clean up Zoom and content syndication data to tell better story
immediate.quote: that'll  just  tell  a  better  story
immediate.speaker: Mani Sandhu

long_term.win: Get to 'pretty happy' status like other Vista portfolio companies (Gainsight, Maples)
long_term.quote: i think here we're almost at the point of breaking into that being pretty happy situation
long_term.speaker: Alex Cox

long_term.win: Use RevSure in Q2 pipeline review calls as meaningful input
long_term.quote: for the first pipeline calls in Q2, I'd love to include at least one RevSure view within the first call. So that's the goal that we are driving toward, something meaningful that we can include in our pipeline calls beginning in Q2.
long_term.speaker: Frank

long_term.win: Prove marketing's value to Vista and Warburg boards with data-driven attribution
long_term.quote: vista likes to grow and they like to grow profitably so um you know that's just uh that's where things are
long_term.speaker: Bryan Urioste

long_term.win: Portfolio company user group to share best practices and accelerate value
long_term.quote: one idea that we're thinking of is like a user group within the Portcos where we're all comfortable with each other. The business is going to be slightly different, but you'll be able to learn from the game sites who are really happy.
long_term.speaker: Alex Cox

before_state.pain_summary: Limited to single-touch lead source attribution, unable to see full customer journey or properly attribute multi-channel influence on pipeline and revenue

after_state.with_revsure: Multi-touch attribution with full journey visibility, but still refining filters and channel classification to match business logic
wins_realized: new business filtering across all dashboards
Direct and chat channel touch creation
Weekly optimization rhythm with RevSure team
Started building Salesforce campaign attribution structure
Identified specific data quality issues to fix
open_issues: opportunity type filter still being fixed
content asset categorization from GA not yet implemented
client team still learning how to interpret and action insights
GA/HubSpot page visit stitching for pre-form journey
Form submit migration gap from initial implementation
Zoom incorrectly counted as page visits
Salesforce campaigns not fully built out with RevSure
